entries.scope: IA-64 ACPI table and platform transaction checks through qtest.
limitations: Transactions are not yet mapped to platform catalogue rows and citations.

entries.scope: IA-64 machine keyboard and input-routing transactions through qtest.
limitations: Transactions are not yet mapped to platform catalogue rows and citations.

entries.scope: Static integrity, citation, implementation-surface, executable-probe, and independent-oracle validation for the atomic ALAT lifecycle contracts.
limitations: Metadata validation supports but does not replace any of the five executable ALAT results.

entries.scope: Focused independent empty-reset field image for all persistent ALAT entries, the replacement cursor, and every transient valid mask, GR mask, and GR reference count from a nonzero dirty preimage; compiler padding is excluded.
limitations: The isolated host image is paired with a separate E2 real-machine system-reset row.

entries.scope: Focused independent ALAT v1 VMState matrix: all legal GR/FR widths and boundary targets, both address modes, exact persistent-field round trip, zero transient summaries, and incoming/outgoing rejection of every invalid semantic-field partition, cursor boundary, and unsupported version.
limitations: QEMU's generic VMState layer owns truncated framing and transport I/O failures; fresh-process guest-visible ALAT safety is a separate E2 row.

entries.scope: Host-side bundle extraction and template invariants.
limitations: Host helper checks do not prove guest-visible bundle execution.

entries.scope: Implementation, catalogue, evidence, and registration closure joins.
limitations: Construction-time closure reporting does not execute conformance cases.

entries.scope: Persistent command batching, binary result and heartbeat queues, generation isolation, and host failure classification.
limitations: The minimal runner self-test validates infrastructure and does not make a normative architectural claim.

entries.scope: One exact executable matrix for every correctness lesson named by construction-order step 7, including special modes and fresh-process migration.
limitations: This representative regression lock does not replace section 10.3 exhaustive variants or make new normative row-closing claims.

entries.scope: Exact plan-lesson membership, executable-probe mapping, callable existence, and limitation validation for construction-order step 7.
limitations: Static matrix validation does not execute the guest probes or close architectural rows.

entries.scope: Guest-executed 637-program IA-64 data-plane base shard, including exact integer NaTPage deferral, no-recovery miss/alignment/protection/debug/VHPT matrices, recovery-model DCR conjunction matrices, PSR.ic=0 always-defer, first-restart PSR.ed software deferral, and the complete 96-program non-faulting integer speculation-attribute matrix.
limitations: Only the explicitly claimed integer PSR.ic=0, PSR.ed, non-faulting speculation-attribute, NaTPage, no-recovery, and recovery-model DCR rows have complete normative contracts and independent exact matrices; broader speculation domains remain candidates.

entries.scope: Guest-executed 42-program ALAT shard: target/type/bank/address/size identity, local store/atomic and explicit invalidation, and true/false nonzero-predicate integer advanced loads.
limitations: Remote invalidation, cache removal, broader floating predication, migration, and malformed state remain separate work; later rows close applicable local context and interruption sequences.

entries.scope: Guest-executed 206-program ALAT shard: every integer physical-alias width pair, every ordinary-store byte-overlap partition, successful and failed XCHG/CMPXCHG/FETCHADD matrices, every GR rotating size, rotating FR, and stacked call-frame remapping.
limitations: Remote invalidation, cache-line removal, ptc.ga, cmp8xchg16, floating-width breadth, migration, replay, debug, and malformed ALAT state remain separate work; later rows close applicable local context and interruption sequences.

entries.scope: Guest-executed 96-program ALAT/RSE shard: every legal equal SOF/SOL size, all 96 physical stacked-register slots, all gcd-derived wrap-cycle lengths, mandatory spill/fill value and NaT round trips, exact backing addresses, and stale-tag rejection after physical reuse.
limitations: Remote invalidation, cache-line removal, ptc.ga, floating-width breadth, migration, replay, debug, and malformed ALAT/RSE state remain separate work; later rows close BSPSTORE context replacement and applicable interruption/RFI coherency.

entries.scope: Guest-executed three-program ALAT software-coherency shard: CLRRRB register remap, local virtual-to-physical DTC remap, and a real involuntary CPL3 timer interruption with handler scratch-tag contamination and typed RFI.
limitations: The normative platform is single-processor, so remote-store/semaphore and remote ptc.ga ALAT coherence are not applicable; cache-line removal, DMA, nested interrupts, selective system-call invalidation, and migration remain separate work.

entries.scope: Data-plane translator source structure and open-audit checks.
limitations: Source structure alone does not prove guest-visible semantics.

entries.scope: Host-side decoder tables, masks, aliases, and malformed encodings.
limitations: Decoder unit coverage does not execute guest-visible instruction semantics.

entries.scope: Direct guest execution of the exact template, L+X, legality, field, IP/RI, resume, and translation-boundary matrices.
limitations: The direct probe batch is diagnostic; row closure is owned by the normative tranche wrapper.

entries.scope: Independent E2 evidence for long-immediate, illegal-placement, field, IP/RI, resume, and translation-boundary rows, plus partial template-equivalence coverage.
limitations: BND-004 remains partial: defined-template acceptance does not independently prove every M/I/F/B/L/X unit mapping with unit-specific observable effects.

entries.scope: Exact production/test template equality, MLX RI=2 guard ordering, section 10.1 metadata, stable identities, citations, and executable-probe validation.
limitations: Static metadata and source validation does not close rows without the E2 runtime gate.

entries.scope: Production binary and source checks for retired execution-engine seams.
limitations: Engine-role structure is not an architectural expected-value oracle.

entries.scope: Evidence-level, lane, CI-tier, registration, and path classification checks.
limitations: Evidence metadata validation does not execute the classified tests.

entries.scope: Combined independent E0 pack/extract and observable E2 execution for bundle format, plus exact E2 sequencing, issue-group, constant-register, commitment, and repair/retry rows.
limitations: BND-001-TEMPLATE-MAP remains partial until every unit mapping and stop form has a unit-specific observable oracle; the other ten claims do not imply profile-wide conformance.

entries.scope: Complete section 10.1 case metadata, citation, stable identity, lane, and independent-oracle validation.
limitations: Static metadata validation is supporting E0 evidence and does not close architectural rows by itself.

entries.scope: Guest-executed IA-64 floating-point programs and result checks.
limitations: Expected values are not yet audited as an independent normative floating-point oracle.

entries.scope: Floating-point translator source and harness structure checks.
limitations: Source structure alone does not prove floating-point results.

entries.scope: Broad guest-executed IA-64 core instruction and state programs, including exact independent PFS call capture and return restoration matrices over direct, long, indirect, predicated, suppressed, clean-frame, incomplete-frame, trap, SoftMMU fill-fault/retry, and fresh-process continuation paths; BR.RET-origin and valid-IFS-RFI-origin current-frame first-register and post-prefix RNAT faults additionally migrate at their handler RFI, restore exact interruption/RSE state, retain poisoned committed words, execute typed RFI, and equal the direct terminal snapshots. Dedicated ALAT programs carry live typed tags through GDB plus fresh-process save/load, execute a real HMP system reset with loader-independent RAM continuity and mandatory stale-tag recovery, and require one recording plus two fresh replays to produce exact terminal architectural equality.
limitations: Only the explicitly enumerated ALAT lifecycle, PFS, and current-frame migration rows are row-closing claims; other broad legacy cases still lack row IDs, printed citations, and full fault/retry metadata.

entries.scope: Core translator source-structure and admission checks.
limitations: Source scans are regression guards rather than architectural execution evidence.

entries.scope: Implementation-derived opcode, register, machine, and QOM inventory.
limitations: Implementation inventory is explicitly not a normative oracle.

entries.scope: Host instruction-helper and internal state invariants.
limitations: Host helper behavior alone does not prove normal guest execution.

entries.scope: Independent literal E2 oracles for code invalidation, architectural RAM TB-boundary equivalence, update safepoints, fault suppression, and exactly-once MMIO rows.
limitations: The claims close only the five explicitly catalogued memory-boundary rows; the remaining width, alignment, speculation, alias, DMA, watchpoint, and address-boundary requirements remain open.

entries.scope: Complete section 10.1 metadata, citation equality, stable identity, executable probe, and independent-oracle validation for five memory-boundary rows.
limitations: Static metadata validation is supporting E0 evidence and does not close rows without the E2 runtime gate.

entries.scope: Host MMU, interruption, priority, and saved-state invariants.
limitations: Direct host state injection is not guest repair-and-retry evidence.

entries.scope: Guest-executed memory, continuation, and fault-boundary programs.
limitations: Cases lack structured normative assertion and repair/retry records.

entries.scope: Normative catalogue schema and semantic validation plus cited Intel manual, printed-page, and normalized-anchor verification.
limitations: Catalogue validation checks citations and assertions as data but does not execute architectural behavior.

entries.scope: Typed opcode authority and implementation ledger drift detection.
limitations: The implementation ledger is not a specification-derived expected-value source.

entries.scope: Host opcode family, side-effect, and fault-trait invariants.
limitations: Implementation trait consistency is not guest-visible semantics.

entries.scope: Partial E2 lane goldens and selected legality, predication, alias, NaT, reserved-field, and slot checks for six broad packed-integer rows.
limitations: The broad rows remain partial until their declared boundary, predicate, NaT, slot, reserved-field, fault-priority, commitment, and repair/retry matrices are complete or split into atomic rows.

entries.scope: Complete section 10.1 metadata, citation equality, stable identity, executable probe-set, and independent-oracle validation for six packed-integer rows.
limitations: Static metadata validation is supporting E0 evidence and does not close rows without the E2 runtime gate.

entries.scope: Guest-executed packed integer and same-group state programs.

entries.scope: Packed-integer translator structure and group-state checks.
limitations: Source structure alone does not prove packed instruction results.

entries.scope: Static integrity, citation equality, stable identity, implementation-surface mapping, executable-probe, and independent-oracle validation for the three atomic PMU selector and PAL discovery contracts.
limitations: Metadata validation supports but does not replace the E2 selector and PAL runtime results.

entries.scope: Vibtanium platform source wiring and declaration checks.
limitations: Static wiring checks do not transact with guest-visible devices.

entries.scope: Independent E2 closure of BR-006 through all 35 loop truth-table rows, a 49-rotation RRB/PR/SOR and NaT wrap, false/no-stop and first-taken controls, and exact illegal-slot 0/1 collection followed by RFI to a legal slot-2 copy; partial independent E2 oracles cover the other seven predicate and six branch rows.
limitations: The other thirteen broad rows remain partial pending complete boundary, predicate, NaT, slot, privilege, fault-priority, commitment, and repair/retry matrices; BR-005, BR-007, BR-010, and PRD-003 are also outside this tranche.

entries.scope: Complete section 10.1 metadata, citation equality, stable identity, executable probe-set, and independent-oracle validation for fourteen predicate and branch rows.
limitations: Static metadata validation is supporting E0 evidence and does not close rows without the E2 runtime gate.

entries.scope: Production-cutover source guards plus focused guest runtime smoke cases.
limitations: Mixed structural and smoke coverage lacks row-level normative metadata.

entries.scope: Independent E2 complete-index and anti-alias matrices for writable GR, FR, PR, and BR indices; every AR selector in both units and directions with enabled/false qualification, exact access-class faults, ignored-register behavior, and CPUID/RUC consistency; nine writable named ARs through 81 reserved-field faults, 512 legal singleton/boundary normalization cases, per-register NaT writes, RSE-mode priority, and RSC privilege clamping; all 64 empty-dirty BSPSTORE offsets plus all 6,208 dirty-size/RNAT-offset combinations through exact BSP/BspLoad rebasing; 332 legal ALLOC field/destination vectors, overlapping value/NaT and same-group frame effects, exact maximum mandatory spill traffic, thirteen Illegal Operations, and one rename-base Reserved Register/Field fault; exact COVER frame/IFS preservation in both PSR.ic states, CLRRRB and CLRRRB.PR rename effects, zero-distance LOADRS invalidation, a 96-register replacement LOADRS payload/RNAT reconstruction, and three precise LOADRS legality faults; one exact 96-register flushrs backing-store image with completed and partial RNAT collections; four BREAK/RFI interrupted-context switches at empty, immediate-RNAT, complete-collection, and maximum-frame boundaries using Intel's exact switch-away and return sequences, disjoint equal-offset backing stores, unchanged interrupted memory, and complete value/NaT/CFM/RSE oracles; a three-return synchronous round trip across two disjoint backing stores using the complete RSC/BSP/PFS/FLUSHRS/lazy/RNAT/INVALA sequence, with independent payload, completed-RNAT, partial-RNAT, ALAT-recovery, and final-frame oracles; guest-visible ALLOC, FLUSHRS, and LOADRS Alternate Data TLB faults after strict mandatory-word prefixes, physical-handler DTC repair, preserved PSR.rt, poison-sensitive RFI retry, and exact fresh-process migration at each repaired deferred-fault boundary; thirteen FLUSHRS/LOADRS Page Not Present, NaTPage, key, access-right, access-bit, and dirty-bit faults with exact PTE/PKR repair and poison-sensitive RFI retry; thirteen two-fault protection-to-Data-Debug priority chains plus FLUSHRS-store and LOADRS-load PSR.dd one-reference suppression cases, all with exact DBR state, interruption metadata, and poison-sensitive retry; FLUSHRS and LOADRS mandatory references through short-format VHPT hits, invalid-entry Data TLB retry, and missing-backing VHPT Translation retry with exact IHA/ITIR and poison-sensitive continuation; four serialized-IC-zero FLUSHRS/LOADRS Data Nested repairs from alternate and VHPT-enabled misses, preserving all eleven outer interruption resources, consuming a register-convention address, directly retrying without prefix replay, and retaining the original outer RFI context; exact CCV effects through twenty cmpxchg1/2/4/8 acquire/release match/mismatch/qualification cases; all 64 address-selected UNAT spill/fill effects with same-group distinct-bit updates and false qualification; every CR selector and direction with enabled/false qualification plus all CR16-CR27 PSR.ic access states, read-only IVR/IRR behavior, per-selector safe-value readback, and a complete-file delayed anti-alias pass; all 23 writable named CRs through 211 reserved-field faults and 1,290 legal singleton/boundary normalization cases plus predicate, NaT, privilege, and PSR.ic priority; all legal GR SOR sizes; complete FR and PR rotating regions; and interruption/RFI selection and preservation of both banked-GR value/NaT images.
limitations: FR0, FR1, GR0, and PR0 use the dedicated REG-004 rows; other named AR and CR state-transition, effect, reset, RSE unaligned/unsupported-attribute and remaining translation fault classes, interrupt-boundary, externally distinguishable in-progress migration phases, and remaining migration semantics, optional ITO advertisement, and remaining RSE-mode restrictions remain separate REG/RSE/MMU/EXC/SYS units; compare-exchange and spill/fill address, translation, access, ordering, dependency, and fault matrices remain separate MEM/MMU/EXC units; optional IFA unimplemented-address checking and PTA undefined alignment are outside the selected profile; nested interruption and MP bank visibility are not claimed by the bank-switch row; arithmetic, disabled-FR, and undefined/newly-allocated RSE value behavior remain separate closure units.

entries.scope: Register tranche schema, coverage-group selectors, exact index counts, complete AR selector/unit/form partitions, exact nine-register named-AR field partitions with 81 fault, 512 legal, and per-register NaT cases, the 6,208-case dirty BSPSTORE/RNAT matrix, exact 96-register flushrs image, synchronous switch and four-boundary interrupted-context switch contracts, mandatory RSE DTC/protection/debug/VHPT/Data-Nested retry plus deferred-DTC and current-frame-fill fault migration metadata, the exact twenty-case CCV/cmpxchg and 64-selector UNAT spill/fill matrices, complete CR selector/read-only/PSR.ic partitions, exact 23-row named-CR field partitions with 211 fault and 1,290 legal cases, CPUID/RUC feature consistency metadata, anti-alias vector completeness, all legal GR SOR sizes, complete FR/PR rotating-base counts, and interruption/RFI transition metadata.
limitations: Static vector completeness does not replace guest execution.

entries.scope: Host RSE frame, backing-store, RNAT, and malformed-state invariants.
limitations: Host RSE state manipulation does not prove guest interruption recovery.

entries.scope: RSE source ownership and helper-boundary structure checks.
limitations: Source structure alone does not prove RSE architectural behavior.

entries.scope: Binary runner protocol schema plus IVT, short-VHPT, translation, and repair/retry builder invariants.

limitations: Implementation inventory is not a normative oracle; these broad rows remain partial until all declared boundary, immediate, alias, predicate, NaT, slot, reserved-field, fault-priority, commitment, and repair/retry cases exist or are split into atomic rows.

entries.scope: Exact translator/test opcode inventory, section 10.1 metadata, citations, stable identities, executable probes, and independent-oracle validation for eight scalar rows.
limitations: Static inventory and metadata validation does not close rows without the E2 runtime gate.

entries.scope: Guest-executed scalar integer model, XMA, character/bit, and state-legality probes.
limitations: The direct probe batch is diagnostic; row closure is owned by the normative tranche wrapper.

entries.scope: Guest-executed IA-64 system, interruption, MMU, and RSE programs, including independent all-256-selector PMC and PMD matrices plus exact PAL_PERF_MON_INFO success and invalid-argument buffers.
limitations: The three cited PMU rows have atomic contracts; legacy system programs outside those rows still lack per-case catalogue, citation, and restart metadata, and PMU event counting remains unimplemented and unclaimed.

entries.scope: System translator source and helper-boundary structure checks.
limitations: Source scans do not prove interruption or MMU semantics.

entries.scope: Fresh-process TCP migration with guest-visible state comparison.
limitations: Round-trip state checks are not yet attributed to individual normative state rows.

limitations: In-process VMState checks do not replace fresh-process migration evidence.

limitations: Broad BIT checks are not yet split into catalogue-cited service rows with exact effect metadata.
